segments.text: Truly God is good to Israel, to those who are pure in heart.
segments.speaker: Asaph

segments.text: But as for me, my feet had almost stumbled, my steps had nearly slipped.
For I was envious of the arrogant when I saw the prosperity of the wicked.
segments.speaker: Asaph

segments.text: For they have no pangs until death; their bodies are fat and sleek.
They are not in trouble as others are; they are not stricken like other men.

footnotes: in the trouble of other men

segments.text: Therefore pride is their necklace; violence covers them as a garment.
Their eyes swell out through fatness; their hearts overflow with follies.

footnotes: have passed the thoughts of the heart

segments.text: They scoff and speak with malice; loftily they threaten oppression.
They set their mouths against the heavens, and their tongue struts through the earth.
Therefore his people turn back to them, and find no fault in them.
And they say, 'How can God know? Is there knowledge in the Most High?'
segments.speaker: Wicked People

segments.text: Behold, these are the wicked; always at ease, they increase in riches.
All in vain have I kept my heart clean and washed my hands in innocence.
segments.speaker: Asaph

segments.text: For all the day long I have been stricken and rebuked every morning.
segments.speaker: Asaph

segments.text: If I had said, 'I will speak thus,' I would have betrayed the generation of your children.
segments.speaker: Asaph

segments.text: But when I thought how to understand this, it seemed to me a wearisome task,
segments.speaker: Asaph

segments.text: until I went into the sanctuary of God; then I discerned their end.
Truly you set them in slippery places; you make them fall to ruin.
How they are destroyed in a moment, swept away utterly by terrors!
Like a dream when one awakes, O Lord, when you rouse yourself, you despise them as phantoms.
When my soul was embittered, when I was pricked in heart,
segments.speaker: Asaph

segments.text: I was brutish and ignorant; I was like a beast toward you.
segments.speaker: Asaph

segments.text: Nevertheless, I am continually with you; you hold my right hand.
segments.speaker: Asaph

segments.text: You guide me with your counsel, and afterward you will receive me to glory.
segments.speaker: Asaph

segments.text: Whom have I in heaven but you? And there is nothing on earth that I desire besides you.
segments.speaker: Asaph

segments.text: My flesh and my heart may fail, but God is the strength of my heart and my portion forever.
segments.speaker: Asaph

segments.text: For behold, those who are far from you shall perish; you put an end to everyone who is unfaithful to you.
But for me it is good to be near God; I have made the Lord God my refuge, that I may tell of all your works.
segments.speaker: Asaph
